_meta.note: Role edges for top-ranked zero-role event hubs; 4 Sonnet proposers, 4 Haiku fresh-verifiers (233C/5A/6P), orchestrator adjudication dropped 8 (incl. R3-29 harm-gate catch missed by verifier).

edges.quote: There sits the only king I mean to bow my knee to, m’lords,” he thundered. “The King in the North!
edges.note: Acclaimed King in the North by the Greatjon and the northern/river lords (Stark side).

edges.quote: All hail His Grace, Joffrey of the Houses Baratheon and Lannister, the First of his Name, King of the Andals and the Rhoynar and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm
edges.note: Crowned king on the Iron Throne (Lannister/crown side).

edges.quote: I am the rightful king, and your son no less a traitor than my brother here.
edges.note: Self-proclaimed king by right of birth (Stannis's own side, Dragonstone).

edges.quote: If I am not safe in the heart of Lord Caswell’s castle, with my own host around me
edges.note: Self-proclaimed king with the great southron host at Bitterbridge (Renly's own side). [S212 adjudication: quote upgraded to Renly claiming his own host]

edges.quote: I am the Greyjoy, Lord Reaper of Pyke, King of Salt and Rock, Son of the Sea Wind, and no man gives me a crown.
edges.note: Declares himself King of the Iron Islands (ironborn/Greyjoy side).

edges.quote: In the dawn light, the army of Lord Tywin Lannister unfolded like an iron rose, thorns gleaming.
edges.note: Commands the westerlands host for the crown (Lannister side); not a claimant king but the war's principal field commander on that side.

edges.quote: Rainbow lights danced once more about the High Septon’s crown as he solemnly declared Joffrey of the Houses Baratheon and Lannister and Margaery of House Tyrell to be one flesh, one heart, one soul.
edges.note: Performs the wedding rite.

edges.quote: “With this kiss I pledge my love!” Joffrey declared in ringing tones.
edges.note: Groom at his own wedding feast.

edges.quote: When Margaery echoed the words he pulled her close and kissed her long and deep.
edges.note: Bride at the wedding.

edges.quote: Tyrion listened with half a ear, as he sampled sweetcorn fritters and hot oatbread baked with bits of date, apple, and orange, and gnawed on the rib of a wild boar.
edges.note: Guest at the feast; later falsely accused of poisoning Joffrey (that accusation/death has its own node, not this wedding).

edges.quote: “My lady.” Tyrion offered Sansa his arm. She took it dutifully, but he could feel her stiffness as they walked up the aisle together.
edges.note: Guest, escorted by her husband Tyrion.

edges.quote: Cersei embraced Margaery and kissed her cheeks.
edges.note: Guest of honor, queen mother.

edges.quote: Lord Tywin did the same, and then Lancel and Ser Kevan.
edges.note: Guest of honor; "the same" refers to embracing/kissing Margaery (previous clause).

edges.quote: Lord Tywin almost always chose to command the reserve; he would take the high ground and watch the battle unfold below him, committing his forces when and where they were needed most.
edges.note: Overall Lannister commander, holds the reserve.

edges.quote: His uncle would lead the center.
edges.note: Commands the Lannister center (archers, pikemen, heavy horse).

edges.quote: Ser Addam Marbrand had the command.
edges.note: Commands the Lannister right wing (cavalry).

edges.quote: Ser Gregor waved his huge sword and bellowed a command, and a thousand other voices screamed back at him.
edges.note: Commands the Lannister vanguard (including Tyrion's clansmen).

edges.quote: The larger part of the northern host, pikes and archers and great masses of men-at-arms on foot, remained upon the east bank under the command of Roose Bolton.
edges.note: Commands the northern foot host (Stark side) in the battle.

edges.quote: A man-at-arms thrust at his chest and his axe lashed out, knocking the spear aside.
edges.note: Fights in the vanguard.

edges.quote: Bronn was surrounded by three foes, but he lopped the head off the first spear that came at him, and raked his blade across a second man’s face on his backslash.
edges.note: Fights beside Tyrion in the vanguard.

edges.quote: Lord Hornwood is dead, and I fear Roose Bolton has escaped us.
edges.note: Killed fighting on the Stark side.

edges.quote: Lords great and small had flocked to Robert’s banners
edges.note: Rebel leader; personally kills Rhaegar at the Trident (rebel side). [S212 adjudication: quote upgraded to banners-flocking command evidence]

edges.quote: “I came down on Storm’s End to lift the siege,” Ned told them, “and the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne dipped their banners, and all their knights bent the knee to pledge us fealty.
edges.note: Rebel commander who lifts the siege of Storm's End (rebel side).

edges.quote: When the Mad King Aerys II Targaryen had demanded their heads, the Lord of the Eyrie had raised his moon-and-falcon banners in revolt rather than give up those he had pledged to protect.
edges.note: Instigator/commander who first raises the rebellion (rebel side, Vale).

edges.quote: “Your brother Rhaegar brought as many men to the Trident,” Ser Jorah admitted,
edges.note: Commands the loyalist host at the Trident (crown/loyalist side).

edges.quote: So His Grace commanded his alchemists to place caches of wildfire all over King’s Landing.
edges.note: King who orders the defense/destruction of King's Landing (crown/loyalist side); did not personally fight this act, hence COMMANDS_IN rather than AGENT_IN.

edges.quote: Wounded by arrow, spear, and sword at the Battle of the Trident whilst fighting beside his Sworn Brothers and Rhaegar Prince of Dragonstone.
edges.note: Kingsguard combatant, loyalist side.

edges.quote: As his men died around him, Littlefinger slid Ned’s dagger from its sheath and shoved it up under his chin.
edges.note: Overpowered and captured at knifepoint by Littlefinger, ending the chapter.

edges.quote: As his men died around him, Littlefinger slid Ned’s dagger from its sheath and shoved it up under his chin. His smile was apologetic. “I did warn you not to trust me, you know.
edges.note: Personally betrays and physically subdues Ned, completing the arrest (matches existing sub-beat littlefinger-betrays-ned).

edges.quote: “You condemn yourself with your own mouth, Lord Stark,” said Cersei Lannister. “Ser Barristan, seize this traitor.
edges.note: Orders Ned's seizure (crown/Lannister side; matches existing sub-beat cersei-orders-ned-s-arrest).

edges.quote: “Kill him!” the boy king screamed down from the Iron Throne. “Kill all of them, I command it!
edges.note: Orders Ned and his men killed from the Iron Throne.

edges.quote: “Men of the Watch!” Janos Slynt shouted, donning his helm. A hundred gold cloaks leveled their spears and closed.
edges.note: Commands the City Watch that turns on Ned's household guard (matches existing sub-beat gold-cloaks-betray-ned).

edges.quote: Janos Slynt himself slashed open Varly’s throat.
edges.note: Personally kills Varly during the melee, distinct from his COMMANDS_IN role over the Watch.

edges.quote: Sandor Clegane’s first cut took off Cayn’s sword hand at the wrist; his second drove him to his knees and opened him from shoulder to breastbone.
edges.note: Kills Cayn, one of Ned's household guard, during the arrest.

edges.quote: Sandor Clegane’s first cut took off Cayn’s sword hand at the wrist; his second drove him to his knees and opened him from shoulder to breastbone.
edges.note: Killed by Sandor Clegane during the melee.

edges.quote: With a single sharp thrust, the nearest gold cloak drove his spear into Tomard’s back.
edges.note: Killed by a gold cloak's spear (agent unnamed in text).

edges.quote: Janos Slynt himself slashed open Varly’s throat.
edges.note: Killed by Janos Slynt.

edges.quote: Daenerys Targaryen wed Khal Drogo with fear and barbaric splendor in a field beyond the walls of Pentos, for the Dothraki believed that all things of importance in a man’s life must be done beneath the open sky.
edges.note: Bride.

edges.quote: there Dany was seated beside Khal Drogo, above the seething sea of Dothraki.
edges.note: Groom.

edges.quote: Viserys was seated just below her, splendid in a new black wool tunic with a scarlet dragon on the chest.
edges.note: Guest of honor, bride's brother.

edges.quote: Illyrio and Ser Jorah sat beside him.
edges.note: Guest, host who arranged the match.

edges.quote: Illyrio and Ser Jorah sat beside him.
edges.note: Guest of honor, place near the khal's bloodriders.

edges.quote: The king himself marched northwest, to the Gods Eye and the newly completed Harrenhal,
edges.note: Leads the central Targaryen thrust against Harrenhal in person.

edges.quote: With them went Queen Visenya and Vhagar.
edges.note: Commands the fleet thrust toward Gulltown/the Vale with Daemon Velaryon.

edges.quote: Queen Rhaenys accompanied him, astride Meraxes of the golden eyes and silver scales.
edges.note: Commands the southern thrust toward Storm's End alongside Orys Baratheon.

edges.quote: The greater part of his host crossed the Blackwater Rush, making south for Storm’s End under the command of Orys Baratheon.
edges.note: Field commander of the host marching on Storm's End.

edges.quote: Once inside, he sent every man of his to the parapets, armed with spears and bows and crossbows, promising lands and riches to whichever of them could bring the dragon down.
edges.note: Opposing king, commands the defense of Harrenhal.

edges.quote: As Argilac the Arrogant gathered his swords at Storm's End, pirates from the Stepstones descended on the shores of Cape Wrath to take advantage of their absence,
edges.note: Opposing king, mobilizes to defend Storm's End against Orys Baratheon.

edges.quote: King Mern had brought half again as many men to the battle as King Loren, and so demanded the honor of commanding the center.
edges.note: Opposing king, commands the allied westerlands/Reach center at the Field of Fire; dies there.

edges.quote: King Loren and his knights would form the right, Lord Oakheart the left.
edges.note: Opposing king, commands the westerlands right wing at the Field of Fire; survives and later bends the knee.

edges.quote: Torrhen Stark, King in the North, had crossed the Neck and entered the riverlands, leading an army of savage northmen thirty thousand strong.
edges.note: Opposing king, leads the northern host to the Trident before submitting without battle (the King Who Knelt).

edges.quote: Prince Daemon would lead the assault on Harrenhal, riding Caraxes.
edges.note: Leads the black assault on Harrenhal in person.

edges.quote: its elderly castellan Ser Simon Strong (uncle to the late Lord Lyonel, greatuncle to Lord Larys) was quick to strike his banners when Caraxes lighted atop Kingspyre Tower.
edges.note: De facto green commander/castellan of Harrenhal in Larys Strong's absence; surrenders the castle.

edges.quote: His Grace commanded “all leal lords” to take the field and disperse the Faith's ragged hordes by force.
edges.note: Crown side; orders the lords of the realm to put down the Faith Militant.

edges.quote: At the behest of the Dowager Queen, they took command of the thousands of Targaryen loyalists in the city and surrounded the Hill of Rhaenys.
edges.note: Crown side; directs the Kingsguard and loyalists against the Warrior's Sons after Maegor's Trial of Seven.

edges.quote: seven hundred knights in silvered steel led by their grand captain, Ser Damon Morrigen, called Damon the Devout.
edges.note: Faith Militant side; grand captain of the Warrior's Sons.

edges.quote: There nine thousand Poor Fellows under Wat the Hewer found themselves caught between six lordly hosts as they attempted to cross the Mander.
edges.note: Faith Militant side; leads the Poor Fellows host destroyed at Stonebridge/Bitterbridge.

edges.quote: By then Ser Horys Hill had reached the Great Fork of the Blackwater with an even larger host; close on thirteen thousand Poor Fellows,
edges.note: Faith Militant side; leads the larger Poor Fellows host at the Great Fork against King Maegor.

edges.quote: Styr had lost all hope of taking Castle Black unawares when Jon escaped him, yet even so, he need not have warned of his approach so bluntly.
edges.note: Wildling/Thenn side; joint commander of the raiding force sent to take Castle Black from the south.

edges.quote: Jarl was with the Magnar; Mance had given them the joint command.
edges.note: Wildling side; joint commander with Styr of the force that scales the Wall.

edges.quote: I suppose I’m as much a commander as we have now.
edges.note: Night's Watch side; de facto commander of Castle Black's defense.

edges.quote: Jon got off three arrows for every bolt Deaf Dick discharged,
edges.note: Fights as an archer in the defense.

edges.quote: He found Ygritte sprawled across a patch of old snow beneath the Lord Commander’s Tower, with an arrow between her breasts.
edges.note: Killed by an arrow in the fighting; shooter not named in text (honest-whodunit, no AGENT_IN proposed).

edges.quote: all the queer wild folk Mance had gathered to break the Wall.
edges.note: Wildling king; commands the main host besieging the gate at Castle Black.

edges.quote: Donal Noye turned toward the two great trebuchets that Bowen Marsh had restored to working order. “Give me light!” he roared.
edges.note: Night's Watch side; commands the defense until he goes down to hold the tunnel gate.

edges.quote: “Load the trebuchet with caltrops,” Jon said. “Owen, Kegs, angle the catapults toward the center.
edges.note: Takes command of the defense atop the Wall after Noye goes down into the tunnel.

edges.quote: Noye’s sword was sunk deep in the giant’s throat, halfway to the hilt.
edges.note: Kills Mag the Mighty in the gate tunnel.

edges.quote: “The giant crushed his spine. I don’t know who died first.”
edges.note: Dies in the tunnel fighting Mag the Mighty.

edges.quote: “It was Mag the Mighty. The king of the giants.”
edges.note: Killed by Donal Noye's sword in the tunnel (mutual kill with Noye).

edges.quote: “Open the south gate,” she commanded, even as the north gate shook to the impact of the ram.
edges.note: Ironborn side; commands the defense and breakout from Deepwood Motte.

edges.quote: Stannis had smashed Mance Rayder’s wildlings at the Wall and cleaned Asha and her ironborn out of Deepwood Motte;
edges.note: Commands the northern/Night's Watch-allied force that takes Deepwood Motte back.

edges.quote: Tris had gained the saddle, and his mount was rearing and wheeling as he laid about with his sword.
edges.note: Ironborn combatant.

edges.quote: Qarl stood over him, with his longsword dripping and moonlight shining in his eyes.
edges.note: Ironborn combatant, fights beside Asha.

edges.quote: A heartbeat later another bush killed Quenton, driving an axe into the base of his skull.
edges.note: Killed in the ambush in the wolfswood after fleeing Deepwood Motte.

edges.quote: Ser Tyland Lannister, recently returned from Myr, was made Hand of the King,
edges.note: Hand of the King, handles day-to-day rule during the regency; existing MEMBER_OF-style edges don't capture his active governing role.

edges.quote: whilst Lord Leowyn Corbray was named Protector of the Realm.
edges.note: Protector of the Realm, responsible for defense during the regency.

edges.quote: The most influential of those was plainly the Sea Snake, whose wealth, experience, and alliances made him the first amongst equals.
edges.note: First among equals of the regents until his death in 132 AC; overlaps with the existing MEMBER_OF edge but reifies his active governing influence.

edges.quote: Yet it was Grand Maester Munkun who put an end to the debate when he said, “My lords, it makes no matter. They are both girls.
edges.note: Grand Maester and regent throughout; actively shapes council decisions (succession debate).

edges.quote: Lord Corlys's seat upon the council of regents had already been offered to an older and more seasoned man: Unwin Peake,
edges.note: Joins the regency council after Corlys's death; later becomes Hand.

edges.quote: but Edmure had thrown his reserve at them, and they had shattered and reeled away bloody and beaten.
edges.note: riverlands/Riverrun side, defended the Red Fork crossings against Tywin

edges.quote: Lord Tywin had tried to force a crossing at a dozen different fords, her brother wrote, but every thrust had been thrown back.
edges.note: westerlands/Lannister side, tried to force the Red Fork

edges.quote: but the fiercest battle had been fought at Stone Mill, where Ser Gregor Clegane had led the assault.
edges.note: Lannister side, led the Stone Mill ford assault (sub-command under Tywin); repelled by Edmure's reserve

edges.quote: We did not cross half the world to wait. Our best chance is to strike hard and fast, before King’s Landing knows who we are. I mean to take Storm’s End.
edges.note: Golden Company/Aegon side, originates and orders the attack

edges.quote: I want the attack to go ahead … with one change. I mean to lead it.
edges.note: Golden Company/Aegon side; insists on personally leading the assault over Connington's plan

edges.quote: Lord Stannis and a small garrison had held the castle for close to a year, against the great host of the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne.
edges.note: Baratheon/rebel side, defender/castellan

edges.quote: Cloaked in that darkness, Davos the smuggler had dared the Redwyne cordon and the rocks of Shipbreaker Bay alike.
edges.note: logistical/supply role, ran the blockade with food that kept the garrison alive; not a combatant

edges.quote: “I came down on Storm’s End to lift the siege,” Ned told them, “and the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne dipped their banners, and all their knights bent the knee to pledge us fealty.
edges.note: Baratheon/rebel side, led the relief force that broke the siege

edges.quote: who had once held Storm’s End through a year of siege, surviving on rats and boot leather while the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne sat outside with their hosts, banqueting in sight of his walls.
edges.note: besieging side (Reach host); surname reference "Lord Tyrell" was Mace Tyrell at this date, corroborated by node Origins text

edges.quote: who had once held Storm’s End through a year of siege, surviving on rats and boot leather while the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne sat outside with their hosts, banqueting in sight of his walls.
edges.note: besieging side, commanded the Redwyne fleet that closed Shipbreaker Bay; surname reference corroborated by node Origins text

edges.quote: He’d only taken the black after he’d lost an arm at the siege of Storm’s End.
edges.note: wounded (arm lost to festering axe wound) during the siege, defending side

edges.quote: before I could reach a decision Lord Balon Greyjoy rose in rebellion against the Usurper, and Ned Stark called his banners to help his friend Robert.
edges.note: rebel/ironborn side, self-proclaimed king

edges.quote: when the stag and the direwolf had joined to end the pretensions of the self-proclaimed King of the Iron Islands.
edges.note: crown side; "the stag" = Robert

edges.quote: before I could reach a decision Lord Balon Greyjoy rose in rebellion against the Usurper, and Ned Stark called his banners to help his friend Robert.
edges.note: crown side, allied commander who called his own banners to aid Robert

edges.quote: Prince Jaehaerys and his sisters, Rhaena and Alysanne, descended upon the castle on their dragons.
edges.note: arrives at King's Landing on dragonback ahead of Rogar's host, takes the Red Keep's holdouts prisoner

edges.quote: A fortnight later, Lord Rogar Baratheon and Queen Alyssa arrived at King’s Landing with their host, and hundreds more were seized and imprisoned.
edges.note: leads the host that follows up Jaehaerys's dragon-arrival and completes the takeover

edges.quote: We will take them under cover of this darkness.
edges.note: Targaryen side, orders the night attack

edges.quote: “Your Grace, I bring you victory. The Stormcrows turned their cloaks, the slaves broke, and the Second Sons were too drunk to fight, just as you said.
edges.note: Targaryen side, commander of Dany's army per node Origins

edges.quote: “If battle is joined, let Grey Worm show wisdom as well as valor,” Dany told him.
edges.note: Targaryen side, Unsullied captain leading the flank assault

edges.quote: Daario upended the sack, and the heads of Sallor the Bald and Prendahl na Ghezn spilled out upon her carpets.
edges.note: killed the other Stormcrow captains and turned the company to Dany's side before the battle began

edges.quote: Daario upended the sack, and the heads of Sallor the Bald and Prendahl na Ghezn spilled out upon her carpets.
edges.note: Stormcrow captain, killed by Daario in the run-up to the battle

edges.quote: Daario upended the sack, and the heads of Sallor the Bald and Prendahl na Ghezn spilled out upon her carpets.
edges.note: Stormcrow captain, killed by Daario in the run-up to the battle

edges.quote: Ser Criston sat on his white horse shouting, “Aim for the rider,” through the smoke and flame.
edges.note: greens side, sprang the trap he had planned

edges.quote: The crimson jaws of Meleys closed round Sunfyre’s golden neck for a moment, till Vhagar fell upon them from above.
edges.note: greens side; Vhagar's dive delivered the fatal blow to Rhaenys/Meleys

edges.quote: A body believed to be Rhaenys Targaryen was later found beside the carcass of her dragon, but it was so blackened that no one could be sure it was her.
edges.note: blacks side, killed with her dragon Meleys

edges.quote: Two more winged shapes appeared: the king astride Sunfyre the Golden, and his brother Aemond upon Vhagar.
edges.note: greens side, rode Sunfyre into the dragon battle and was badly burned

edges.quote: Another hundred perished not long after, when Prince Aemond and Ser Criston Cole took Rook's Rest and put its garrison to death.
edges.note: castellan of Rook's Rest, killed with his garrison after the dragon battle

edges.quote: I took this castle and I mean to hold it, to live or die as Prince of Winterfell.
edges.note: ironborn/defending side

edges.quote: I have near two thousand men with me . . . and if the tales be true, you have no more than fifty.
edges.note: Stark side, castellan leading the relief force

edges.quote: When the old fool gave me his hand, I took half his arm instead. Then I let him see my face.
edges.note: killed during the parley by Ramsay (Red Helm); distinct from his COMMANDS_IN role earlier in the same battle

edges.quote: When the old fool gave me his hand, I took half his arm instead. Then I let him see my face.
edges.note: Ramsay's own confession of killing Rodrik Cassel during the parley, disguised as "Reek"/Red Helm

edges.quote: Ser Rodrik seemed to have the numbers, but the Dreadfort men were better led, and had taken the others unawares.
edges.note: led the Dreadfort relief force (as "Red Helm") that broke the northern siege of Winterfell; distinct combat-command role from the AGENT_IN killing of Rodrik

edges.quote: With Leobald Tallhart and Cley Cerwyn.” The boy lord had taken an arrow in the eye
edges.note: young Lord Cerwyn, killed in the battle

edges.quote: With Leobald Tallhart and Cley Cerwyn.
edges.note: commanded a Tallhart contingent in the relief force, among the dead corpses brought to the gates

edges.quote: Sixteen years ago, a bastard son of King Aegon IV named Daemon Blackfyre had risen in revolt against his trueborn brother.
edges.note: rebel/Blackfyre side

edges.quote: His revolt had ended on the Redgrass Field, where Daemon and his twin sons died beneath a rain of Lord Bloodraven's arrows.
edges.note: loyalist side, commanded the archers that broke the rebellion at Redgrass Field

edges.quote: Sixteen years ago, a bastard son of King Aegon IV named Daemon Blackfyre had risen in revolt against his trueborn brother.
edges.note: loyalist/crown side; Daeron II is the "trueborn brother" whose throne was contested

edges.quote: His head was smashed in by a mace wielded by a lord with three castles on his shield." Lord Gormon Peake.
edges.note: rebel/Blackfyre side, killed Roger of Pennytree at Redgrass Field with his mace

edges.quote: Shawney was with Bittersteel on the right, and almost died of his wounds.
edges.note: rebel/Blackfyre side ("Bittersteel"), commanded the right flank of Daemon's host at the Battle of the Redgrass Field; escaped into exile afterward

edges.quote: Caggo was the one who finally cut him down, fighting through the king’s protectors on his monstrous warhorse and opening Cleon the Great from shoulder to hip with one blow of his curved Valyrian arakh.
edges.note: Windblown/Yunkai-allied side, killed Cleon in the sortie battle beneath the walls

edges.quote: Frog had done his part in the slaughter that followed, riding down the frightened eunuchs with the other Windblown.
edges.note: disguised as "Frog," squire to Greenguts, fighting in the Windblown company

edges.quote: The three Dornishmen spilled from the tent together to join the fighters sprinting for the horse lines.
edges.note: disguised as "Dornish Gerrold," fighting in the Windblown company

edges.quote: The three Dornishmen spilled from the tent together to join the fighters sprinting for the horse lines.
edges.note: disguised as "Greenguts," fighting in the Windblown company

edges.quote: I built a fleet at Robert’s command, took Dragonstone in his name.
edges.note: led the fleet and conquest of Dragonstone

edges.quote: I built a fleet at Robert’s command, took Dragonstone in his name.
edges.note: orderer/instigator; commanded Stannis to build the fleet and take the island but did not personally execute it

edges.quote: one night Ser Willem Darry and four loyal men had broken into the nursery and stolen them both, along with her wet nurse, and set sail under cover of darkness for the safety of the Braavosian coast.
edges.note: smuggled Viserys and infant Daenerys off Dragonstone before Stannis's fleet arrived, denying him his prize

edges.quote: In the end, the second Daemon Blackfyre rode forth alone, reined up before the royal host, and challenged Lord Bloodraven to single combat.
edges.note: rebel/Blackfyre side, styled himself Daemon II Blackfyre, King

edges.quote: Instead, Lord Bloodraven's men surrounded him, pulled him off his horse, and clasped him into golden fetters.
edges.note: loyalist side, Hand of the King, commanded the royal host that surrounded Whitewalls and captured Daemon

edges.quote: Lord Peake himself was storming across the muddy field to climb the steps to the herald's platform, his cloak flapping behind him. "We are betrayed!" he cried.
edges.note: rebel side, chief instigator/orchestrator of the Whitewalls conspiracy; node already carries an in:SUSPECTED_OF edge from this event, this COMMANDS_IN is a distinct confirmed role, not a duplicate

edges.quote: "I told you." Dunk stabbed him through the throat. "I'm better with a sword."
edges.note: killed Ser Tommard Heddle in single combat when Heddle tried to seize Prince Aegon ("Egg") for the conspirators

edges.quote: "I told you." Dunk stabbed him through the throat. "I'm better with a sword."
edges.note: conspirator, commanded Lord Butterwell's garrison; harm-gate note: this node is event.war (VICTIM_IN not permitted here) even though the text shows Heddle killed in single combat by Duncan the Tall -- routed to FIGHTS_IN per SHARED-RULES harm-gate guidance rather than forcing VICTIM_IN onto the war umbrella

edges.quote: That was where Robert had made his breach, swarming in over the rubble and corpses with his warhammer in hand and Ned Stark at his side.
edges.note: crown side, personally led the breach assault

edges.quote: That was where Robert had made his breach, swarming in over the rubble and corpses with his warhammer in hand and Ned Stark at his side.
edges.note: crown side, fought at Robert's side in the breach

edges.quote: Theon had watched from the safety of the Sea Tower, and sometimes he still saw the torches in his dreams, and heard the dull thunder of the collapse.
edges.note: child at the time, watched the battle (including his brother Maron's death) from the Sea Tower; the perception itself is load-bearing (recurring nightmare)

edges.quote: Rodrik had been slain by Lord Jason Mallister at Seagard, Maron crushed in the collapse of the old south tower
edges.note: killed when the south tower collapsed during Robert's breach

edges.quote: The final battle was on Pyke. When Robert’s stonethrowers opened a breach in King Balon’s wall, a priest from Myr was the first man through, but I was not far behind. For that I won my knighthood.
edges.note: crown side, won his knighthood for valor in the breach

edges.quote: When the walls of Pyke crashed down, he was the first through the breach. He fought with one of his flaming swords, setting ironmen afire with every slash.
edges.note: crown side, first man through the breach, wielding a wildfire-coated sword

edges.quote: where Robert had accepted the rebel lord’s surrender and Ned had taken his son Theon as hostage and ward
edges.note: ironborn/defending side, surrendered his fallen stronghold to Robert

edges.quote: He had extinguished the proud Reynes of Castamere and the ancient Tarbecks of Tarbeck Hall root and branch when he was still half a boy.
edges.note: Lannister side; Tyrion's retrospective attributes the destruction campaign directly to Tywin (line 367).

edges.quote: He had extinguished the proud Reynes of Castamere and the ancient Tarbecks of Tarbeck Hall root and branch when he was still half a boy.
edges.note: House Reyne destroyed root and branch (line 367); battle subtype passes harm-gate (destruction).

edges.quote: He had extinguished the proud Reynes of Castamere and the ancient Tarbecks of Tarbeck Hall root and branch when he was still half a boy.
edges.note: House Tarbeck destroyed root and branch (line 367).

edges.quote: When Tarbeck Hall came crashing down on Lady Ellyn, that scheming bitch, Tyg claimed he smiled then.
edges.note: Ellyn (Reyne by birth, Lady Tarbeck by marriage) named directly as the hall is destroyed on her (line 273).

edges.quote: Sandog tore Viserys's battleaxe from the wood where the prince had buried it and split Ser Amaury's head and helm in half from crest to gorget.
edges.note: Kills Ser Amaury Peake at the drawbridge of Maegor's Holdfast (line 263, spelled 'Sandog' in this OCR pass). event.incident node_type bars VICTIM_IN for Amaury Peake — see R3-06 (FIGHTS_IN) instead.

edges.quote: Yet Peake fought on with stubborn valor, and near the end, for half a heartbeat, the gods gave him his chance
edges.note: Leads the guard assault on the holdfast and is killed there (line 259); routed to FIGHTS_IN not VICTIM_IN — event.incident harm-gate excludes VICTIM_IN despite the death.

edges.quote: “If you are come to take my lady wife, ser, turn and go,” the young prince said, “for you shall not pass whilst I still stand.”
edges.note: Personally blocks the drawbridge with a battle-axe to defend Larra Rogare (line 217).

edges.quote: | commanded Ser Amaury to be gone, and he refused.
edges.note: King commands the holdfast's defiance during the secret siege (line 271; '|' for 'I' is an OCR artifact, kept verbatim).

edges.quote: The army Lord Alyn was to carry to the Vale would be commanded by Lady Larra’s brother Moredo Rogare, Lord Rowan announced.
edges.note: Commands the Vale relief army during the Gilded Falcon/Mad Heir succession crisis, the same crisis year as the secret siege (line 141).

edges.quote: The rest of the Red Keep was in the hands of Ser Marston Waters and his Kingsguard, whilst beyond the castle walls Ser Lucas Leygood and his gold cloaks kept a firm grip on King’s Landing.
edges.note: New Hand commands the besieging force during the eighteen-day secret siege of Maegor's Holdfast (line 267).

edges.quote: Daenerys Targaryen eased herself onto the chair’s plush velvet seat, and Hizdahr zo Loraq went to his knees, unlaced her sandals, and washed her feet
edges.note: Bride at the Temple of the Graces ceremony (line 347); matches existing graph convention (bride/groom = AGENT_IN on ceremony hubs, cf. wedding-ceremony-at-the-great-sept-of-baelor).

edges.quote: Four hours later, they emerged again as man and wife, bound together wrist and ankle with chains of yellow gold.
edges.note: Groom (line 351).

edges.quote: Hand in hand, they followed the Green Grace inside the temple, where the air was thick with incense and the gods of Ghis stood cloaked in shadows in their alcoves.
edges.note: The Green Grace leads the couple into the temple rite (line 349); Galazza is the named Green Grace throughout this arc.

edges.quote: “Magnificence, you do not understand,” protested Reznak. “The washing of the feet is hallowed by tradition.
edges.note: Organizes/negotiates the wedding customs and preparations with Daenerys (line 127) — administrative, non-combat.

edges.quote: I think we should attack from three sides. Grey Worm, your Unsullied shall strike at them from right and left, while my kos lead my horse in wedge for a thrust through their center.
edges.note: Targaryen side, overall commander of the campaign (line 207).

edges.quote: When she had commanded the Unsullied to choose officers from amongst themselves, Grey Worm had been their overwhelming choice for the highest rank.
edges.note: Targaryen side; commands the Unsullied (line 39).

edges.quote: Leading her van, Daario had given orders for the children to be taken down before Dany had to see them
edges.note: Targaryen side; commands the Stormcrows in the van of the march (line 33).

edges.quote: unlike Ser Jorah, Daario, Brown Ben, and her three bloodriders, the eunuch did not lead troops, plan battles, or give her counsel.
edges.note: Targaryen side; text confirms Jorah leads troops and plans battles for Dany (line 55).

edges.quote: unlike Ser Jorah, Daario, Brown Ben, and her three bloodriders, the eunuch did not lead troops, plan battles, or give her counsel.
edges.note: Targaryen side; Second Sons captain, already fighting for Dany at this point in ASOS (pre-dates his later ADWD defection to Yunkai, see ben-plumm-defects-to-yunkai) (line 55).

edges.quote: And Dany swept the lash down as hard as she could across the slaver’s face.
edges.note: Personally strikes Kraznys and commands Drogon's fire (line 253).

edges.quote: A lance of swirling dark flame took Kraznys full in the face.
edges.note: Killed by Drogon's fire in the Plaza of Punishment (line 257).

edges.quote: Drogon flew almost lazily at Kraznys, black wings beating.
edges.note: The dragon is the direct executor of Kraznys's death (line 259).

edges.quote: Jhogo’s whip coiled about his neck and cut off his shout.
edges.note: Bloodrider, fights in the plaza chaos (line 259).

edges.quote: Another lost a hand to Rakharo’s arakh and rode off reeling and spurting blood.
edges.note: Bloodrider, fights in the plaza chaos (line 259).

edges.quote: Aggo sat calmly notching arrows to his bowstring and sending them at tokars.
edges.note: Bloodrider, fights in the plaza chaos (line 259).

edges.quote: Strong Belwas had his arakh out as well, and he spun it as he charged.
edges.note: Fights in the plaza chaos (line 259).

edges.quote: And all around them slavers ran and sobbed and begged and died, and the dusty air was filled with spears and fire.
edges.note: Faction node; executes Dany's 'Slay the Good Masters' order (line 271), the mechanism of the betrayal/massacre.

edges.quote: Prince Rhaegar carried her off and raped her,” Bran explained.
edges.note: The dominant in-world/realm framing (Bran's account; Robert's 'how many times do you think he raped your sister?' at agot-eddard-02.md:79 is the same claim from the other side). Node already carries rhaegar-targaryen SUSPECTED_OF abduction-of-lyanna (tier-2, deliberately hedged) — this AGENT_IN reifies the dominant accusatory narrative itself, not a graph assertion of settled fact; the opposing 'died for the woman he loved' counter-frame (agot-daenerys-08.md:187, already cited on the node) is the acknowledged counter-testimony. Flagging the overlap per dedup rule rather than silently duplicating.

edges.quote: the Kingslayer descended on them and put them to flight.
edges.note: Lannister side, commands the force that routs Vance and Piper (line 123).

edges.quote: Lord Vance was slain.
edges.note: Killed holding the pass (line 123).

edges.quote: Lord Piper was falling back to join your brother and his other bannermen at Riverrun, with Jaime Lannister on his heels.
edges.note: Riverlands side, survives and retreats (line 123).

edges.quote: Edmure has commanded Lord Vance and Lord Piper to guard the pass below the Golden Tooth.
edges.note: Riverlands side, orderer of the defense — did not personally fight this engagement (line 15).

edges.quote: A man tried to pull me from the saddle. The Hound killed him, I think . . . his arm . . .
edges.note: Nearly pulled from her horse by the mob (line 133); node_type is literally event.battle so VICTIM_IN passes the gate.

edges.quote: The Hound killed him, I think . . . his arm . . .
edges.note: Kills a rioter defending Sansa, cutting off his arm (line 133).

edges.quote: They were traitors,” Joffrey squealed from the ground. “They called me names and attacked me!”
edges.note: Attacked by the mob, precipitating Sandor's killing (line ~99).

edges.quote: Bronn had a score of sellswords scattered through the crowd with orders to stop any trouble before it started.
edges.note: Acting Hand organizing crowd-control ahead of the riot (line 59) — administrative, not personal combat.

edges.quote: Bronn had a score of sellswords scattered through the crowd with orders to stop any trouble before it started.
edges.note: Deploys sellswords into the crowd at Tyrion's order (line 59).

edges.quote: It is,” Aegon replied eagerly. “If my aunt wants Meereen, she’s welcome to it. I will claim the Iron Throne by myself, with your swords and your allegiance.
edges.note: Personally makes the sail-west decision (line 217).

edges.quote: Prince Aegon,” said Tristan Rivers, “we are your men. Is this your wish, that we sail west instead of east?”
edges.note: Puts the decisive question to Aegon on behalf of the council (line 215).

edges.quote: The last to do so was Homeless Harry Strickland, blistered feet and all.
edges.note: Captain-general; argued for caution but is the last to kneel and pledge, joining the decision organizationally (line 223). Overlaps with the existing harry-strickland OPPOSES edge on this node (he opposed the plan before acquiescing).

edges.quote: Ride back to the Shy Maid and return with Lady Lemore and Ser Rolly. We’ll need Illyrio’s chests as well.
edges.note: Organizes logistics immediately following the decision (line 229).

edges.quote: Daemon was the Warrior himself that day. No man could stand before him. He broke Lord Arryn's van to pieces and slew the Knight of Ninestars and Wild Wyl Waynwood before coming up against Ser Gwayne Corbray of the Kingsguard.
edges.note: Rebel/Blackfyre side, leads the rebel host and personally fights (line 703).

edges.quote: The war was done when Daemon died.
edges.note: Distinct second role, textually supported separately from his command role (line 705).

edges.quote: He slew Aegon first, the elder of the twins, for he knew that Daemon would never leave the boy whilst warmth lingered in his body
edges.note: Loyalist side (King's Hand); personally kills Aegon and Aemon Blackfyre with arrows from Weeping Ridge, turning the battle (line 703).

edges.quote: He slew Aegon first, the elder of the twins, for he knew that Daemon would never leave the boy whilst warmth lingered in his body
edges.note: Killed by Bloodraven's arrows (line 703).

edges.quote: Young Aemon took up Blackfyre when the blade slipped from his dying father's fingers, so Bloodraven slew him, too, the younger of the twins.
edges.note: Killed by Bloodraven immediately after his father and twin (line 703).

edges.quote: the gift Bittersteel had given him upon the Redgrass Field.
edges.note: Bittersteel puts out Bloodraven's eye in this battle (line 51, Dunk's recollection); text also has him 'turning the rout and leading his mad charge' afterward (line 705).

edges.quote: he might have broken Maekar's left before Bloodraven could take the ridge.
edges.note: Loyalist side, commands a wing ('Maekar's left') — his alias 'The Anvil' matches the 'hammer and the anvil' song reference on this node (line 707).

edges.quote: Prince Baelor's hammerblow against the rebel rear, the Dornishmen all screaming as they filled the air with spears
edges.note: Loyalist side, commands the Dornish contingent that strikes the rebel rear (line 705).

edges.quote: You are to harry the Stony Shore, raiding the fishing villages and sinking any ships you chance to meet.
edges.note: Orders the raid (line 399), does not personally execute it.

edges.quote: I mean for you to strike the first blow, Theon. You shall take eight longships north—
edges.note: Given nominal command of the raid party by Balon (line 395); acok-theon-03.md:53 notes the command is contested by his own men.

edges.quote: Down to the pebbled beach they would go, to drown Benfred Tallhart in salt water.
edges.note: Oversees/performs the ritual drowning of the captured Benfred Tallhart (line 39).

edges.quote: Down to the pebbled beach they would go, to drown Benfred Tallhart in salt water.
edges.note: Captured and drowned during the raid (line 39); his death is the node's stated mechanism for concealing the invasion's true size.

edges.quote: Theon had assigned him the task of guarding the ships; otherwise men would have called it Dagmer’s victory, not his.
edges.note: Co-commands the raiding party aboard Foamdrinker (line 65).

edges.quote: At best Jonos Bracken had five hundred men about the castle.
edges.note: Besieging/Iron Throne side; Jonos commands the force starving out Raventree.

edges.quote: Tytos Blackwood has not bent the knee,
edges.note: Defending side; Tytos holds Raventree against Jonos's siege.

edges.quote: I mean to deal with Lord Tytos myself. It won’t require a siege tower.
edges.note: Jaime personally resolves the siege via parley rather than assault.

edges.quote: At the Bridge of Skulls he had met the Weeper and three hundred wildlings and won a bloody battle.
edges.note: Night's Watch side commander.

edges.quote: At the Bridge of Skulls he had met the Weeper and three hundred wildlings and won a bloody battle.
edges.note: Wildling/free folk raiding-party side commander.

edges.quote: More than a hundred brothers slain, among them Ser Endrew Tarth and Ser Aladale Wynch.
edges.note: Killed in the battle.

edges.quote: More than a hundred brothers slain, among them Ser Endrew Tarth and Ser Aladale Wynch.
edges.note: Killed in the battle.

edges.quote: Anguy’s arrow took him in the chest.
edges.note: Opens the battle by killing the rooftop sentinel; overlaps with sub-beat node anguy-kills-the-rooftop-sentinel, reified onto this parent hub.

edges.quote: Kyle and Notch let fly together.
edges.note: Kills a door guard; overlaps with sub-beat kyle-and-notch-kill-the-door-guards, reified onto this parent hub.

edges.quote: Kyle and Notch let fly together.
edges.note: Kills a door guard alongside Kyle; overlaps with sub-beat kyle-and-notch-kill-the-door-guards.

edges.quote: Kyle was trying to nock another shaft to his string when a man in black mail flung a spear through his belly.
edges.note: Kyle is killed mid-battle (distinct beat from his earlier AGENT_IN kill of the door guard).

edges.quote: “Give the dying the gift of mercy and bind the others hand and foot for trial,” he commanded, and it was done.
edges.note: Brotherhood without banners leader; commands the mercy-killing/binding after the fight and the subsequent trial that ends in Utt's hanging.

edges.quote: Thoros and Lord Beric were everywhere, their swords swirling fire.
Arya saw Lem’s bright yellow cloak flapping behind him as he rode down the man who’d killed Kyle.
She spied Ned too, fighting at the lightning lord’s side.
edges.note: ‘Ned’ = Edric Dayne, Beric's young squire (per node prose/wiki identification); the name alone in this single line doesn't disambiguate him from other Neds, hence tier-2.

edges.quote: Lord Beric had left Beardless Dick and Mudge to guard them.
edges.note: Guards Arya and Gendry away from the fighting; non-combat supportive role.

edges.quote: Lord Beric had left Beardless Dick and Mudge to guard them.
edges.note: Guards Arya and Gendry away from the fighting; non-combat supportive role.

edges.quote: Jack-Be-Lucky, Harwin, and Merrit o’ Moontown braved the burning septry to search for captives.
edges.note: Rescue of the captive brown brothers; non-combat.

edges.quote: Jack-Be-Lucky, Harwin, and Merrit o’ Moontown braved the burning septry to search for captives.
edges.note: Rescue of the captive brown brothers; non-combat.

edges.quote: Jack-Be-Lucky, Harwin, and Merrit o’ Moontown braved the burning septry to search for captives.
edges.note: Rescue of the captive brown brothers; non-combat.

edges.quote: Septon Utt soon dangled beneath a tall elm, swinging slowly by the neck, as naked as his name day.
edges.note: Hanged in the post-battle trial that this node's Aftermath section folds into its scope.

edges.quote: Watty fell with a grunt, his bow slipping from his hand.
edges.note: Struck down mid-battle.

edges.quote: Arya watched from atop her horse, on the crest of the wooded ridge that overlooked the septry, mill, brewhouse, and stables and the desolation of weeds, burnt trees, and mud that surrounded them.
edges.note: POV character directly observes the battle from the ridge (not shielded); text-anchor gate satisfied.

edges.quote: Cousin, take this creature to the Wolf’s Den and cut off his head and hands.
edges.note: Per prompt guidance, Wyman is the mastermind/agent of the whole staged deception even though the physical mechanics are delegated.

edges.quote: Cousin, take this creature to the Wolf’s Den and cut off his head and hands.
edges.note: Tasked executor of the staged custody/execution; takes Davos into Wolf's Den keeping. Overlaps with sub-beat wyman-publicly-orders-davos-execution, reified onto this parent hub.

edges.quote: Ser Bartimus saw that the fingers of his left hand were shortened, the same as yours.
edges.note: Mutilates the substitute's hand to sell the deception.

edges.quote: Prince Aemond mounted Vhagar and went after him.
edges.note: Pursues and fights Lucerys, resulting in the killing.

edges.quote: Lucerys Velaryon died with his dragon, Munkun insists.
edges.note: Killed in the dragon-fight/pursuit.

edges.quote: Arrax fell, broken, to be swallowed by the storm-lashed waters of the bay.
edges.note: Lucerys's dragon, killed alongside him.

edges.quote: Vhagar was five times the size of her foe, the hardened survivor of a hundred battles.
edges.note: Aemond's dragon; the one that physically overpowers Arrax.

edges.quote: I saw the Mountain slay Raymun Darry with a single blow so terrible that it took Darry’s arm off at the elbow and killed the horse beneath him too.
edges.note: Harwin's eyewitness account of the ambush.

edges.quote: I saw the Mountain slay Raymun Darry with a single blow so terrible that it took Darry’s arm off at the elbow and killed the horse beneath him too.
edges.note: Killed by Gregor Clegane.

edges.quote: Gladden Wylde died there with him, and Lord Mallery was ridden down and drowned.
edges.note: Killed in the ambush.

edges.quote: Gladden Wylde died there with him, and Lord Mallery was ridden down and drowned.
edges.note: Drowned fleeing the ambush.

edges.quote: Alyn shouted commands and restored order to our ranks, and those still ahorse rallied around Thoros and cut our way free.
edges.note: Rallies the broken king's-men line during the retreat.

edges.quote: Alyn shouted commands and restored order to our ranks, and those still ahorse rallied around Thoros and cut our way free.
edges.note: Survivors rally around him to cut free of the ambush.

edges.quote: He sent Lord Beric instead, with twenty of his own men and twenty from Winterfell, me among them.
edges.note: King's-men/Robert's-justice side; Eddard's chosen commander of the force ambushed at the Mummer's Ford.

edges.quote: He sent Lord Beric instead, with twenty of his own men and twenty from Winterfell, me among them.
edges.note: One of the twenty Winterfell guardsmen in Beric's ambushed force; narrates the battle to Arya as a survivor.

edges.quote: Your Mountain stole my harvest and burned everything he could not carry off. He put my castle to the torch and raped one of my daughters.
edges.note: Jonos Bracken to Jaime, describing the Mountain's men's raid.

edges.quote: Your Mountain stole my harvest and burned everything he could not carry off. He put my castle to the torch and raped one of my daughters.
edges.note: His castle, harvest, and daughter are the harm described.

edges.quote: He smashed the Lords Vance and Piper at the Golden Tooth, and met the massed power of the Tullys under the walls of Riverrun.
edges.note: Tywin recounting Jaime's victory to Tyrion.

edges.quote: Ser Edmure Tully was taken captive, with many of his knights and bannermen.
edges.note: Captured in the defeat.

edges.quote: Lord Blackwood led a few survivors back to Riverrun, where Jaime has them under siege.
edges.note: River-lords side; leads the surviving remnant back to Riverrun.

edges.quote: Ser Gregor’s taken the castle.
edges.note: Tywin reporting to Jaime that Gregor's men retook Harrenhal.

edges.quote: The sellswords fled as soon as they knew we were coming, all but a few. One of the cooks opened a postern gate for us, to get back at Hoat for cutting off his foot.
edges.note: Polliver's own account (‘we were coming’) confirms he was present for the retaking; Sandor's opening question (‘So Gregor took Harrenhal?’) shows Sandor was NOT present, so no Sandor edge is proposed here. [S212 adjudication: tier-2, presence explicit, combat borderline]
